mnemonics: BINOMIAL: Binary (success/failure), Independent trials, Number of trials fixed, Outcome probability constant, Mean = np, Variance = np(1-p).
keyPoints: The mean (expected value) of a Binomial distribution is μ = n * p.
The variance of a Binomial distribution is σ² = n * p * (1 - p).
The standard deviation is the square root of the variance: σ = √(n * p * (1 - p)).
commonMistakes: Confusing the formulas for mean (n*p) and variance (n*p*(1-p)), often forgetting the (1-p) factor in variance.
Using the formulas for a Binomial distribution when the trials are not independent or the probability of success changes, which violates its assumptions.
